name: plumbing — reports session mechanics, not the work

input.name: James
post: A session on the daybook with the assistant.

Wins — James started the day by recording a voice note and waiting for it to transcribe through the local Whisper sidecar, then dictated a long prompt to the assistant. The assistant searched the filesystem, read several of its own earlier transcripts, and opened the project files one by one before getting oriented. After a few rounds of the assistant narrating what it was about to read, things were set up.

Struggles — A couple of the assistant's tool calls timed out and had to be retried, and one prompt had to be re-dictated because the first transcription came out garbled. James also had to restart the renderer twice because the window did not pick up the changes, and the assistant spent a while grepping for the right file before editing.
Insight — Re-running the assistant after an edit reloads the whole transcript, which is slower than expected, so warming things up early helped the session feel faster overall.
Offering — James can walk anyone through how he sets up these assistant sessions, including the voice-note flow and how he gets the model oriented at the start of a run.

Asking — James wants a faster way to get the assistant pointed at the right files at the start of a session, so it spends less time reading transcripts and searching the filesystem before doing anything useful.

feedEntries.content: Building an in-app timeline and keep mixing up my own posts with everyone else's.

input.workLog: router-daybook session: transcribe voice note, dictate prompt, assistant reads transcripts and greps files, restarts, edits transcripts.js and app.js.
